calendar.description: Senate Natural Resources, Agriculture, and Environment Hearing
House Natural Resources, Agriculture, and Environment Hearing

general_provisions: This bill addresses natural resources within the state.
hilighted_provisions: This bill:<hr><ltbullet>facilitates faster permitting by the Department of Environmental Quality and the Division of Oil, Gas, and Mining;<hr><ltbullet>modifies the tax credit for mining exploration;<hr><ltbullet>creates the State Reinvestment Restricted Account and diverts various streams of income related to severance taxes into the State Reinvestment Restricted Account;<hr><ltbullet>addresses property tax differentials based on critical minerals zones;<hr><ltbullet>defines terms;<hr><ltbullet>establishes the state critical minerals objectives and policy, including providing for annual reviews;<hr><ltbullet>creates the Critical Minerals Council (council), including establishing the council's operations, powers, and duties;<hr><ltbullet>makes money appropriated to the council nonlapsing;<hr><ltbullet>addresses areas of coordination by certain council members;<hr><ltbullet>establishes a process to designate critical minerals zones, including providing for property tax differential revenue;<hr><ltbullet>provides for the creation of a clearinghouse of data to be known as the "Critical Minerals Atlas";<hr><ltbullet>addresses the creation of the Minerals for Industrial, National, and Economic Security Center;<hr><ltbullet>creates the Critical Minerals Development Account;<hr><ltbullet>coordinates with H.B. 373, Higher Education Innovation, to address inclusion of critical minerals projects in the eligible research areas for research grants; and<hr><ltbullet>makes technical and conforming amendments.
